rationale: A confirmed exploit chain is evidenced: Apache ActiveMQ (version 5.18.1, a known RCE-vulnerable version) spawned cmd.exe (A0, EV-001), which used certutil to download a randomly-named executable from an internal attacker-controlled host (10.0.2.13:8080) and immediately executed it (EV-003, EV-004, EV-005, EV-006). The dropped payload (qSwUwejx.exe, original_file_name '-' indicating stripped PE metadata) then established a TCP connection to 10.0.2.13:4444 — a classic reverse-shell port — confirming C2 callback (EV-007). The payload subsequently spawned an interactive cmd.exe shell (EV-008, EV-009), and the attacker then used that shell to download AnyDesk via certutil for persistent remote access (EV-010, EV-011, EV-012). Every stage of the kill chain — initial access via vulnerable middleware, payload delivery, execution, C2 establishment, and persistence/lateral-tool staging — is directly evidenced with no benign explanation available.
